title: Albumin-creatinine ratio review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

follow_up.text: Supports ACR, PCR, urinalysis, quantitative confirmation, and interpretation factors including haematuria, menstruation, exercise, and infection.
follow_up.rationale: Supports ACR measurement, quantitative confirmation and interpretation factors for urine albumin-creatinine ratio review; no decision is generated.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports ACR, PCR, urinalysis, quantitative confirmation, and interpretation factors including haematuria, menstruation, exercise, and infection.

source.title: KDIGO 2024 Clinical Practice Guideline for the Evaluation and Management of Chronic Kidney Disease

exact_section.heading: Evaluation of albuminuria
exact_section.locator: Practice Points 1.3.1.1–1.3.1.3 and Table 16; summary PDF pages 39 and 75–76

follow_up.text: Supports clinician-arranged GFR and albuminuria monitoring and evaluation of a greater-than-20-percent eGFR change or ACR doubling without mapping fixed legacy intervals.
follow_up.rationale: Supports clinician-led GFR and albuminuria monitoring in CKD for urine albumin-creatinine ratio review; no decision is generated.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-arranged GFR and albuminuria monitoring and evaluation of a greater-than-20-percent eGFR change or ACR doubling without mapping fixed legacy intervals.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Sample context, value, units, category, prior results, eGFR, diabetes, blood pressure, transient factors, confirmation, diagnosis and plan remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for urine albumin-creatinine ratio review.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
